proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_palbociclib (positive main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_tamoxifen.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_trastuzumab.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with stage_iv=0 (negative effect of stage IV disease).
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without brain metastases.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative monotonic prognostic effect).

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 5.41 mo on palbociclib vs 4.31 mo off (Welch t-test, t=43.5, p<1e-300). Strong positive main effect.
Mean PFS 4.72 mo on tamoxifen vs 4.68 mo off (t=1.77, p=0.077). No main effect.
Mean PFS 4.65 mo on trastuzumab vs 4.71 mo off (t=-2.18, p=0.029). Modest negative main effect (likely confounding by HER2+ being a worse-prognosis subgroup).
Mean PFS 4.66 mo on olaparib vs 4.70 mo off (p=0.33). No main effect.
Mean PFS 4.69 vs 4.69 mo (p=0.85). No main effect.
Mean PFS 4.63 mo on pembro vs 4.70 off (t=-2.38, p=0.018). Small negative main effect.
Mean PFS 3.62 mo (stage IV) vs 5.15 mo (non-stage IV); p<1e-300. Strong negative prognostic effect.
Mean PFS 3.75 mo (brain mets) vs 4.80 mo (no brain mets); p=1.45e-178. Strong negative prognostic effect.
Mean PFS 5.64 mo (ECOG 0), 4.44 mo (ECOG 1), 3.29 mo (ECOG 2). Monotonic decline; ANOVA F p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is larger in patients with her2_positive=1 than in her2_positive=0 (positive treatment_trastuzumab × her2_positive interaction).
treatment_tamoxifen benefit on pfs_months is larger in er_positive=1 than er_positive=0 (positive treatment_tamoxifen × er_positive interaction).
treatment_palbociclib benefit on pfs_months is larger in er_positive=1 than er_positive=0 (positive treatment_palbociclib × er_positive interaction).
treatment_olaparib benefit on pfs_months is larger in patients with brca1_mutation=1 or brca2_mutation=1 (positive treatment_olaparib × brca_any interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in msi_high=1 (positive interaction).
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan benefit on pfs_months is larger in her2_low=1 (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef -0.028, p=0.695. No interaction; trastuzumab does not preferentially benefit HER2+ patients in this dataset.
Interaction coef -0.058, p=0.27. No tamoxifen × ER interaction.
Strong positive interaction: coef +1.594, p=9.3e-229. Palbociclib benefit confined to er_positive=1.
Positive interaction: coef +0.402, p=0.021. Suggests olaparib benefit in BRCA carriers.
Interaction coef +0.217, p=0.466. No detectable pembrolizumab × MSI interaction (small MSI subgroup, n=498).
Interaction coef -0.038, p=0.626. No sacituzumab × HER2-low interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib increases mean pfs_months relative to no palbociclib; in er_positive=0 patients, the difference is null.
Among palbociclib-treated patients, postmenopausal=1 status modifies the palbociclib benefit (treatment_palbociclib × postmenopausal interaction).
treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months in patients with brca_any=1 but not in brca_any=0.
The treatment_olaparib × brca1_mutation interaction is larger than the treatment_olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of palbociclib vs no-palbociclib within ER strata.
analyses.result_summary: ER+: palbo n=12,194 mean 5.89 vs n=22,692 mean 4.31 (diff +1.58, p<1e-300). ER−: palbo n=5,234 mean 4.29 vs n=9,880 mean 4.30 (diff -0.02, p=0.63). Confirms ER-restricted benefit.
Interaction coef -0.134, p=0.0043. Modest negative interaction: palbociclib benefit slightly attenuated in postmenopausal patients (likely confounding with ER status).

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests of olaparib within brca_any strata.
analyses.result_summary: BRCA+: olaparib n=239 mean 4.93 vs no-olaparib n=2,260 mean 4.59 (diff +0.35, p=0.041). BRCA-: diff -0.06, p=0.14. Olaparib benefit confined to BRCA carriers.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib*brca1_mutation', df) and same for brca2
analyses.result_summary: olaparib × BRCA1 interaction coef +0.446, p=0.066. olaparib × BRCA2 coef +0.340, p=0.16. BRCA1 trend stronger but neither reaches significance individually due to small subgroup sizes.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (positive main effect).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative main effect).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative main effect).
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative main effect).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative main effect).
Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Lower hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months (positive correlation hypothesized).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.100, p=9.4e-112. Higher albumin → longer PFS, as hypothesized.
r=-0.016, p=0.00025. Small negative correlation as hypothesized.
r=-0.003, p=0.46. No detectable association.
r=+0.003, p=0.54. No association.
r=-0.091, p=3.0e-93. Higher Ki67 → shorter PFS, as hypothesized.
r=-0.116, p=4.1e-148. More weight loss → shorter PFS.
r=+0.0004, p=0.93. No association.
r=-0.013, p=0.003. Small negative correlation.
r=-0.005, p=0.30. No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with pik3ca_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients without (negative main effect).
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with liver_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
Patients with bone_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.
pleural_effusion=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
chronic_kidney_disease=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
heart_failure=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
autoimmune_disease=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
prior_malignancy=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
met_exon14_skipping=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.33 mo (PIK3CA mut) vs 4.89 mo (PIK3CA WT); diff -0.56, p=6.1e-144. Strong negative main effect.
Mean PFS 4.70 vs 4.69 mo; diff +0.007, p=0.76. No main effect.
Mean PFS 4.71 vs 4.69 mo; p=0.56. No effect.
Mean PFS 4.72 vs 4.69 mo; p=0.17. No effect.
Mean PFS 4.66 vs 4.70 mo; p=0.28. No effect.
p=0.64. No effect.
p=0.67. No effect.
p=0.38. No effect.
p=0.17. No effect.
Mean PFS 4.55 vs 4.70 mo; diff -0.14, p=0.024. Borderline negative effect (small subgroup, n=1,501).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories (private, medicare, medicaid, uninsured).
Patients with rural_residence=1 have shorter pfs_months than urban patients.
Higher education_years is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher smoking_pack_years is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative prognostic effect).
prior_surgery=1 is associated with longer pfs_months (selection of surgically treatable disease).

analyses.result_summary: Cell means: white 4.68, black 4.72, hispanic 4.71, asian 4.74, other 4.76. Overall ANOVA F p=0.49. No race/ethnicity effect.
Cell means: private 4.68, medicare 4.69, medicaid 4.71, uninsured 4.73. ANOVA p=0.82. No insurance effect.
Mean PFS 4.71 (rural) vs 4.69 (non-rural); p=0.34. No effect.
r=-0.004, p=0.39. No association.
r=-0.003, p=0.48. No association.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df.age_years, df.pfs_months) and smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: r=+0.701, p<1e-300; OLS coef +0.176 per year, p<1e-300. Higher age is STRONGLY associated with LONGER PFS in this dataset (opposite to the hypothesized direction). Mean PFS by age bin: <40 →0.56, 40–50 →1.52, 50–60 →3.06, 60–70 →4.67, 70–80 →6.32, 80+ →8.20.
Mean PFS 4.74 (yes) vs 4.67 (no); diff +0.066, p=0.005. Modest positive effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between age_years and pfs_months persists after adjustment for ECOG, stage_iv, brain mets, ki67_pct, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, pik3ca_mutation, and palbociclib×ER (i.e. age effect is not driven by confounding with prognostic features).
At least one snp_rs* genotype is associated with pfs_months (any of the 24 SNP indicator columns) at Bonferroni-adjusted p<0.05/24 ≈ 0.0021.
snp_rs1801131=1 (MTHFR A1298C carrier) is associated with shorter pfs_months — top hit on uncorrected screen.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + C(ecog_ps) + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + treatment_palbociclib*er_positive + ki67_pct + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + pik3ca_mutation', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age coef +0.1756 / year, p<1e-300 (essentially identical to univariate +0.1759). The positive age→PFS association is robust to adjustment for major prognostic covariates and is a dominant feature of the data.

analyses.code: Per-SNP Welch t-tests across all 24 snp_rs* columns
analyses.result_summary: Of 24 SNPs, 1 reached uncorrected p<0.05 (snp_rs1801131, p=0.026); 0 passed Bonferroni threshold of 0.0021. No reproducible SNP main effect on PFS.
snp_rs1801131=1 vs 0: mean PFS 4.66 vs 4.71 mo; diff -0.054, p=0.026. Nominally significant but does not survive multiple-testing correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS of pfs_months on age_years, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, er_positive, her2_positive, pik3ca_mutation, ki67_pct, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and the six treatment indicators with palbociclib×ER and trastuzumab×HER2 interactions, the dominant predictors are age_years (+), ECOG (−), stage_iv (−), has_brain_mets (−), her2_positive (−), pik3ca_mutation (−), albumin (+), ki67_pct (−), weight_loss (−), and the palbociclib×ER interaction (+).
After multivariable adjustment, the marginal effect of treatment_palbociclib in er_positive=1 patients remains a clinically meaningful positive PFS gain (~1.5 mo).
After multivariable adjustment, no treatment_trastuzumab × her2_positive interaction is detectable (interaction p>0.05).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + C(ecog_ps) + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + er_positive + her2_positive + pik3ca_mutation + ki67_pct + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_palbociclib*er_positive + treatment_tamoxifen + treatment_trastuzumab*her2_positive + treatment_olaparib + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R²=0.811 (n=50,000). All hypothesized prognostic variables significant: age_years +0.176/yr, ECOG-1 -1.19, ECOG-2 -2.34, stage_iv -1.55, has_brain_mets -0.98, her2_positive -0.47, pik3ca_mutation -0.58, ki67_pct -0.019/%, albumin +0.48/g·dL⁻¹, weight_loss -0.076/%, palbociclib×ER +1.56 (all p<1e-200). Other treatment main effects and trastuzumab×HER2 interaction non-significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols of pfs_months on covariates+treatment_palbociclib in subset er_positive==1
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib coef in ER+ subgroup = +1.569 mo, p<1e-300, n=34,886. Confirms ER-restricted clinically meaningful benefit.
trastuzumab×HER2 coef +0.011, p=0.71. No interaction, even after multivariable adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of treatment_palbociclib among er_positive=1 patients is attenuated or abolished by pik3ca_mutation=1 (negative treatment_palbociclib × pik3ca_mutation interaction within ER+).
Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib × stage_iv interaction is non-zero.
Within er_positive=1 patients, treatment_palbociclib × age_years interaction is non-zero (palbociclib benefit varies with age).
treatment_pembrolizumab interaction with at least one of msi_high, tp53_mutation, her2_positive, er_positive, pik3ca_mutation, stage_iv, has_brain_mets is significant.
treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan interaction with at least one of her2_low, her2_positive, er_positive, pik3ca_mutation, stage_iv is significant.
Higher fatigue_grade, pain_nrs, dyspnea_grade, cough_grade, or appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within ER+, interaction coef -2.34, p<1e-300. PIK3CA mutation completely abolishes the palbociclib benefit. ER+/PIK3CA-WT palbo n=8,034 mean 6.68 vs no-palbo n=14,754 mean 4.31 (diff +2.38, p<1e-300). ER+/PIK3CA-MUT palbo n=4,160 mean 4.37 vs no-palbo n=7,938 mean 4.33 (diff +0.04, p=0.39).
Interaction coef +0.031, p=0.37. No palbociclib × stage_iv interaction.
Interaction coef -0.0021/yr, p=0.24. No palbociclib × age interaction.

analyses.code: Loop of smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab*<marker>') for marker in [msi_high, tp53_mutation, her2_positive, er_positive, pik3ca_mutation, stage_iv, has_brain_mets]
analyses.result_summary: All interaction p-values ≥0.05 (smallest: pembro × er_positive p=0.050, coef -0.13). No reliable pembrolizumab interaction.
All interaction p-values >0.28. No sacituzumab interaction.
fatigue_grade r=0.0002 (p=0.96), pain_nrs r=-0.002 (p=0.72), dyspnea_grade r=0.005 (p=0.30), cough_grade r=-0.004 (p=0.38), appetite_loss_grade r=-0.001 (p=0.86). No symptom-grade effect on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a significant three-way treatment_palbociclib × er_positive × pik3ca_mutation interaction on pfs_months: palbociclib benefit is largest when er_positive=1 AND pik3ca_mutation=0, and is null in all other strata.

A systematic screen of all six treatments × thirteen binary biomarkers yields exactly two reliable interactions on pfs_months: treatment_palbociclib × er_positive (positive) and treatment_olaparib × brca_any (positive); no other pairwise treatment×marker interaction reaches p<0.01.

A parsimonious multivariable OLS including age_years, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, her2_positive, ki67_pct, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, and the three-way palbociclib×er_positive×pik3ca_mutation term explains ≥80% of variance in pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coef -2.43, p=1.6e-128. Cell means confirm palbociclib benefit is restricted to ER+/PIK3CA-WT (palbo 6.68 vs no-palbo 4.31; +2.38 mo) and absent in all other 3 strata.

analyses.code: Loop smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <treatment>*<marker>') over treatments × markers; collect interactions with p<0.01
analyses.result_summary: When the screen is run on the full cohort, the only interactions surviving p<0.01 are treatment_palbociclib × er_positive (+1.59, p=9e-229) and treatment_palbociclib × pik3ca_mutation (negative, only within the ER+ stratum, see h9). The treatment_olaparib × brca_any interaction is +0.40 (p=0.021), which crosses 0.05 but not the strict 0.01 threshold; remaining 78 treatment-marker pairs are non-significant. Conclusion: the predominant mechanism of treatment heterogeneity is palbociclib's ER+/PIK3CA-WT-restricted benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + C(ecog_ps) + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + her2_positive + ki67_pct + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_palbociclib*er_positive*pik3ca_mutation', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: R²=0.849 (n=50,000). All prognostic terms p<1e-200; palbociclib×ER coef +2.39, three-way interaction with PIK3CA -2.40 (both p<1e-300). The model explains 85% of PFS variance.
